body:
This is a complicated issue, but the best way I got your example to run was: https://github.com/Haroenv/test-next-blitz

1. "dehydrate" metadata (remove functions, since they can't be serialised, and React InstantSearch internals recreates a noon function on render) https://github.com/Haroenv/test-next-blitz/blob/9538da4e40ea6d089ced108b6599661aff149ed9/app/pages/search.tsx#L72-L94
2. "dehydrate" state (turn class into object, and React InstantSearch internals turns it back into the class on first render) https://github.com/Haroenv/test-next-blitz/blob/9538da4e40ea6d089ced108b6599661aff149ed9/app/pages/search.tsx#L96-L102

note that if you have multi-index, resultsState is an array, and you need to do these same things in a loop

3. I had another issue with webpack / events in the underlying helper which was solved by changing the webpack config: https://github.com/Haroenv/test-next-blitz/blob/master/blitz.config.js#L18-L22

related issues: 
- https://github.com/vercel/next.js/issues/11993
- https://github.com/vercel/next.js/pull/12156
- https://github.com/algolia/react-instantsearch/issues/2960
- https://github.com/eggheadio/egghead-next/pull/133

Looks like the first two steps possibly can be done in React InstantSearch directly

Wow thank you for taking the time to work through this. I really appreciate it. I had experimented with excluding the `value` field from serialization but that seemed to turn into other issues which may have been the web pack issues you described. 

This definitely solves my problem in the short term. It would definitely be nice if react instantsearch handled the serialization issues long term.
